text: It's been a little while since my last running video, so today I'm going to go for a run and see what fruits in season, so come with me. A lot of people have been asking how I can get such a good time running while filming all these fruits, and the truth is, for the first couple miles, I just jog or walk. After the warm-up, that's when I try to get my best mile time. The first thing I ended up coming across was the star fruit tree that had started to send out a whole bunch of flower and set a couple little fruit. After that, I came across these two citrus trees that were loaded with fruit, and at first I thought they were grapefruit, but they look a little too big, so I think these are actually pomello. After that, I came across this papaya tree that had sprouted up right on the outside of this fence, and was loaded with fruit, as well as a butterfly pea flower vine growing right beside it. After that, I came across some dragon fruit, also known as pataya, that had climbed all the way up this tree, and then started making fruit. Next, I came across some bananas, as well as some monstera deliciosa fruit, that still have a ways to go before they'll be ripe and ready to pick. And I'm not sure how well you can see this, but this right here is actually a guava tree, and it has a fruit way up at the top of it. I also came across a smaller citrus tree that was loaded, as well. I'm not sure exactly if these are lemons or limes, but still pretty cool to see them. And last but not least, you know it wouldn't be Florida if you didn't see some coconuts. And now that the warm up's done, it's time for the run! I ended up getting my new best mile time of 7 minutes and 18 seconds, which is 22 seconds faster than my previous. I am now officially exhausted. Now I'm gonna go take a much needed shower, and until next time, get outside and see what you can find.
